vuln_q_0020_0002.title: CWE-943 - NoSQL Injection
vuln_q_0020_0002.description: User input is fed into a NoSQL command. This allows a user to execute an NoSQL command injection and run custom actions, which could leak sensitive data or delete data in the database. Ensure the user does not have direct influence on the command.
vuln_q_0020_0002.recommendation: Potential NoSQL Injection detected. Untrusted input is being used in a NoSQL database operation without proper sanitization.  NoSQL databases like MongoDB and DynamoDB are not immune to injection attacks. User input should never be directly interpolated into query objects or strings.  Instead, use parameterized queries, proper sanitization techniques, or type conversion specific to the NoSQL database you're using.  For MongoDB, consider using ObjectId() for IDs and validated operators.  For Dyna

[Learn more](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/862.html)

vuln_q_0097_0004.title: CWE-943 - NoSQL Injection
vuln_q_0097_0004.description: User input is fed into a NoSQL command. This allows a user to execute an NoSQL command injection and run custom actions, which could leak sensitive data or delete data in the database. Ensure the user does not have direct influence on the command.
vuln_q_0097_0004.recommendation: Potential NoSQL Injection detected. Untrusted input is being used in a NoSQL database operation without proper sanitization.  NoSQL databases like MongoDB and DynamoDB are not immune to injection attacks. User input should never be directly interpolated into query objects or strings.  Instead, use parameterized queries, proper sanitization techniques, or type conversion specific to the NoSQL database you're using.  For MongoDB, consider using ObjectId() for IDs and validated operators.  For Dyna

vuln_q_0097_0004.title: CWE-798 - Hardcoded credentials
vuln_q_0097_0004.description: Access credentials, such as passwords and access keys, should not be hardcoded in source code. Hardcoding credentials may cause leaks even after removing them. This is because version control systems might retain older versions of the code. Credentials should be stored securely and obtained from the runtime environment.
vuln_q_0097_0004.recommendation: We detected credentials hardcoded in your code, which might allow unauthorized users to access your account. We recommend you to store your credentials outside of the code in a configuration file, a database, or a management service for secrets and retrieve from it. For more information, see [CWE-259](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/259.html) and [CWE-798](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/798.html).

vuln_q_0019_0001.title: CWE-77,78,88 - OS command injection
vuln_q_0019_0001.description: Constructing operating system or shell commands with unsanitized user input can lead to inadvertently running malicious code.
vuln_q_0019_0001.recommendation: OS command injection occurs when untrusted input is used directly in system commands or shell operations. This vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary commands on the host system. To prevent OS command injection, use secure alternatives like the `subprocess` module with `subprocess.run()` and pass arguments as a list instead of shell=True, or use proper input validation with proper allowed characters. For more information about OS command injection, visit https://owasp.org/www-commun

vuln_q_0068_0004.title: CWE-400,664 - Resource leak
vuln_q_0068_0004.description: Allocated resources are not released properly. This can slow down or crash your system. They must be closed along all paths to prevent a resource leak.
vuln_q_0068_0004.recommendation:
**Problem**
This line of code assigns a resource that might leak to a class field, causing a resource leak that slows down or crashes your system. For example, when *Connection.cursor()* throws an exception, the *SecureDatabase* object isn’t constructed so you can’t close this resource afterward.

**Fix**
Ensure that if an exception is thrown, the *connection* resource is closed before the constructor is exited.

vuln_q_0017_0001.title: CWE-352 - Cross-site request forgery
vuln_q_0017_0001.description: Insecure configuration can lead to a cross-site request forgery (CRSF) vulnerability. This can enable an attacker to trick end users into performing unwanted actions while authenticated.
vuln_q_0017_0001.recommendation: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) protection is disabled or missing. This vulnerability allows attackers to trick users into performing unwanted actions on your site when they're authenticated. Enable CSRF protection by using framework-specific mechanisms like Django's @csrf_protect decorator, validating CSRF tokens in Flask routes, or using middleware-based solutions for FastAPI/Starlette. Learn more - https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/352.html

vuln_q_0040_0002.title: CWE-352 - Cross-site request forgery
vuln_q_0040_0002.description: Insecure configuration can lead to a cross-site request forgery (CRSF) vulnerability. This can enable an attacker to trick end users into performing unwanted actions while authenticated.
vuln_q_0040_0002.recommendation: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) protection is disabled or missing. This vulnerability allows attackers to trick users into performing unwanted actions on your site when they're authenticated. Enable CSRF protection by using framework-specific mechanisms like Django's @csrf_protect decorator, validating CSRF tokens in Flask routes, or using middleware-based solutions for FastAPI/Starlette. Learn more - https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/352.html

vuln_q_0000_0003.recommendation:
Your code is logging sensitive information through print statements, potentially exposing credentials, personal data, or tokens. This poses a security risk, as such logs may be accessible via console output or log files. To remediate, mask sensitive values and use appropriate logging levels to protect user privacy and meet security regulations.

[Learn more](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/200.html)
